events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: voltage glove class wrong or overdue. Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Electrical PPE wrong class or overdue can be critical. Let's decide high or critical. Voltage gloves wrong class could expose to shock; overdue means degraded. Critical maybe because life safety. But maybe high. We'll choose critical. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant, worn/degraded? Overdue implies degraded/worn. Use installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded. percent_complete maybe 80. value irrelevant 0. findings: voltage-rated gloves class does not match task voltage; gloves are past test date; no valid certification label. actions: remove from service, replace with correct class and in-date gloves, verify testing date, train worker. references: NFPA 70E 130.7(C)(1), OSHA 1910.137. Need include order/workable empty. event_time 0. rate 0. sound_source "". confidence 0.95.

  "findings": [
    "Voltage-rated rubber insulating gloves have the wrong class for the exposed voltage",
    "Gloves are past the required periodic electrical test date",
    "No valid in-service certification/date marking visible on the gloves"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the incorrect or overdue gloves from service",
    "Issue rubber insulating gloves with the correct voltage class for the task",
    "Verify gloves are within the 6-month electrical test interval and marked",
    "Reinspect remaining PPE and retrain affected workers on glove selection"
  ],
  "rationale": "Electrical protective gloves must be the correct voltage class and current on periodic testing; wrong-class or overdue gloves cannot provide assured protection against electric shock, creating a life-safety hazard before close-out.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E",
    "OSHA 1910.137",
    "OSHA 1910 Subpart I"
  ]
}
